ORDER AND STIPULATION FOR MOTION TO BE HEARD BY A MEMBER OF THE CALIFORNIA BAR SERVING AS A TEMPORARY JUDGE

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. MR. SCOTT REPRESENTED TO THE COURT THAT THERE WAS A DECLARATION FROM OPPOSING COUNSEL THAT HE IS NOT OPPOSING THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND WILL PROVIDE THE COURT BY WAY OF E-MAIL A COPY OF SUCH DECLARATION. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF CIT TECHNOLOGY FINANCING SERVICES, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - THE MOTION IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 18, 2013, TO GIVE THE OPPOSING PARTY A CHANCE TO COMPLY WITH SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6B. THE OPPOSING PARTY SHALL PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR FILINGS BY MARCH 11 WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARINGS DATE. THE COURT PREVIOUSLY ORDERED THE OPPOSING PARTY TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES ON FEBRUARY 22, 2013. THE OPPOSING PARTY DID NOT COMPLY WITH THAT ORDER. IF THE COURT DOES NOT RECEIVE THE COURTESY COPIES FOR THE NEW HEARING DATE, IT WILL REVIEW THE MERITS OF THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WITHOUT THE OPPOSITION. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. PLAINTIFF CIT TECHNOLOGY FINANCING SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF MAINTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION BASED ON THE HAINES DECLARATION. DEFENDANT FAILED TO FILE ANY SUBSTANTIVE OPPOSITION AND THE GENERAL ASSIGNMENT FOR BENEFIT OF CREDITORS DOES NOT STAY PROSECUTION OF THIS MATTER. THE COURT SIGNED THE ORDER AND THE JUDGMENT THAT WERE EMAILED TO THE COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-18-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark M. Scott, Esq. of Buchalter Nemer, Tel. 949.224.6217, for plaintiff and moving party by Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: The court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF CIT TECHNOLOGY FINANCING SERVICES, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Plaintiff maintained its initial burden of production based on the Haines declaration. Defendant failed to file any substantive opposition and the General Assignment for Benefit of Creditors does not stay prosecution of this matter. The Court signed the order and the judgment that were emailed to the court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-07-2013 Continued To Mar-18-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARK M. SCOTT OF THE BUCHALTER, NEMER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (949) 224-6217 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. MR. SCOTT REPRESENTED TO THE COURT THAT THERE WAS A DECLARATION FROM OPPOSING COUNSEL THAT HE IS NOT OPPOSING THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND WILL PROVIDE THE COURT BY WAY OF E-MAIL A COPY OF SUCH DECLARATION. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CIT TECHNOLOGY FINANCING SERVICES, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - The motion is continued to March 18, 2013, to give the opposing party a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The opposing party shall provide courtesy copies of their filings by March 11 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearings date. The Court previously ordered the opposing party to provide courtesy copies on February 22, 2013. The opposing party did not comply with that order. If the Court does not receive the courtesy copies for the new hearing date, it will review the merits of the motion for summary judgment without the opposition. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
